titleStmt.title: SII 28.225: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 225. (A.R. No. 380 of 1913.)

summary.p: Eṟumbūr, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
Kadambavanēśvara temple, central shrine, south base.

Portions of the inscription are damaged. It records the endowment for a sandhi lamp by a manṟādi residing in ••• ••• ••• ••• maṅgalam, a brahmadēyam in Vaḍakarai Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu. Three śivabrahmaṇas holding kāṇi in the temple took over what was endowed (the nature of the material endowed is not clear from the extant readable portion of the inscription-probably 12 sheep were endowed) and undertook the responsibility of maintaining the lamp.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tribhuvaṉaccakkaravarttikaḷ śrīirājairāja devaṟkku yāṇṭu Eḻāvatu vaṭakarai virutarāja payaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭup piramateyam
Uṟumurāṉa vikkiramacoḻaccaruppeti maṅkalattirukkum manṟāṭi tirukkaḻi pāla veṉa vakkon pakkal Ivvūr ti
t tirukkoyil nāyanāṟku canti viḷak panniraṇṭum kaikko
ṇṭom Ikkoyil kāṇi Uṭaiya civappirāmaṇa comatevapaṭṭanum kāciyapan tiruveṇṇāma
lai Uṭaiyān paṭṭanāna tirucciṟṟampala paṭṭanenum pārattuvāci cūriyatevan Āṭkkoḷḷavantān paṭṭanenum Immuvom Ivan pa
kkal canti viḷakku kaikko
ṇṭom Immuvomum I
ppaṭikku Ivai kavucikan co
mateva paṭṭan Eḻuttu Ippaṭikku
Ivai kāciyapan tiruve
malai Uṭaiyān paṭṭanāna tiA few letters at the end of these lines have been lost.
ṟampalapaṭṭan EḻuttuAn unconnected damaged piece in the place inscribed in characters of the 12th century contains six lines, of which only nikarili coḻa in line 4 is clearly readable.
Digital edition of SII 28.225 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
